title: trias flava
intro: Trias flava, also known as yellow gentian or bitterwort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the gentian family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bitter compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including indigestion, constipation, and bloating, as well as to stimulate the appetite and improve liver function. The health benefits of Trias flava include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Trias flava can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
